etymology.description: Depicts a bolt of lightning -> original character for 電(電) (diàn, "lightning") and 神(神) (shén, "god")
etymology.interpretation: The character 申(shēn, "to extend") visually represents a bolt of lightning, capturing the idea of something that strikes and extends rapidly across the sky. This image strongly aligns with the concept of extension and rapid expansion, hence its meaning as "to extend."

examples.chinese: 他把手臂伸出窗外(他把手臂伸出窗外)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ shǒubì shēn chū chuāngwài.
examples.english: He extended his arm out of the window.

examples.chinese: 老师让学生们把腿伸直(老師讓學生們把腿伸直)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī ràng xuéshēngmen bǎ tuǐ shēn zhí.
examples.english: The teacher asked the students to straighten their legs.

examples.chinese: 在练习中，我们要学会如何伸展身体(在練習中，我們要學會如何伸展身體)。
examples.pinyin: Zài liànxí zhōng, wǒmen yào xuéhuì rúhé shēnzhǎn shēntǐ.
examples.english: In practice, we must learn how to stretch our bodies.
